name: Cardiogen
subtitle: AEDR | Cardiovascular Bioregulator Peptide

molecular.halfLife: Minutes (short peptide); effects persist via epigenetic changes

overview: Cardiogen is a Khavinson bioregulator peptide consisting of four amino acids, derived from heart tissue research. Unlike larger peptides that work on cell surfaces, Cardiogen penetrates directly to the cell nucleus where it binds to specific DNA regions to regulate gene expression. This epigenetic mechanism supports tissue repair and improves cardiovascular function, particularly benefiting the heart and blood vessels.
mechanism: Cardiogen works through epigenetic regulation by penetrating cell nuclei and interacting with specific DNA regions, histones, and nucleosomes. It regulates which genes are activated or suppressed without changing DNA sequence. This mechanism supports cardiac tissue repair, enhances myocardial function, and improves cardiovascular tissue regeneration by influencing gene expression in heart cells.
keyBenefits: Supports cardiac tissue repair and regeneration

items.name: Cardiac Tissue Repair
items.description: Supports heart tissue repair through gene expression regulation at the nuclear level.

items.name: Myocardial Function
items.description: May improve overall myocardial function and cardiac performance.

items.name: Cardiovascular Aging
items.description: Addresses age-related cardiovascular decline through bioregulation.

items.name: Epigenetic Regulation
items.description: Influences gene expression to support tissue repair without altering DNA sequence.

items.name: Tissue Regeneration
items.description: Promotes cardiac tissue regeneration at the cellular level.

deliveryMethods.overview: Available in capsule form for oral administration. Short peptides can be absorbed orally and reach target tissues. Typical protocol involves 10-20 day cycles, often repeated 2-3 times per year.
Injectable form available for direct administration, bypassing GI tract.

interactions.notes: Complementary cardiovascular bioregulators - Cardiogen for heart, Vesugen for vessels.
Often combined in comprehensive anti-aging Khavinson protocols.
Different organ targets; can be used in comprehensive bioregulator protocols.
Part of Khavinson bioregulator family; targets different tissue.

timeline.effects: Effects persist due to epigenetic changes

references.title: Peptide Regulation of Gene Expression: A Systematic Review
references.keyFindings: Short peptides can penetrate nuclei, interact with DNA and histones to regulate gene expression.

references.title: Cardiogen Bioregulator Research
references.journal: Bulletin of Experimental Biology and Medicine
references.keyFindings: Demonstrated effects on cardiac tissue through gene expression modulation.

references.title: Khavinson Peptide Bioregulators Overview
references.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of bioregulator peptides and their epigenetic mechanisms.

quickStats.storage: Capsules: room temperature; Injectable lyophilized: 2-8°C; Reconstituted: 2-8°C refrigerated
